results.title: Instruments of the band and orchestra
results.brief_description: A series of five 10 minute instructional films of the different musical sections which make up an orchestra.

results.title: The brasses
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Instruments of the band and orchestra”.
Explains the contribution of the brass section in bands and orchestras. Explains the principle of sound production in the brass instruments. Includes demonstrations of the trumpet, cornet, french horn, trombone, baritone and tuba.
results.description:

group.title: Instruments of the band and orchestra

results.title: The Woodwinds
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Instruments of the band and orchestra”.
Explains that the woodwind instruments produce interesting and colorful sounds in bands and orchestras. Shows how woodwinds are held and played and explains the basic principle of sound production in these instruments. Includes demonstrations of the flute, fife, piccolo, clarinet, saxophone, oboe and bassoon.

group.title: Instruments of the band and orchestra

results.title: The Strings
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Instruments of the band and orchestra”.
Presents the instruments which comprise the string section of an orchestra and shows various musical effects which are obtained from the violin, viola, cello, string bass, and harp. Explains the principles of sound production in string instruments and shows how the violin is played. Explains that the piano is a string instrument which is often used in solo position with an orchestra.

group.title: Instruments of the band and orchestra

results.title: The Percussions
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Instruments of the band and orchestra”.
Explains that percussion instruments emphasise rhythm and provide special sound effects in marching bands, concert bands and orchestras. Includes demonstrations of the bass drum, snare drum, field drum, tympani, cymbals, tambourine, temple blocks, triangle, castanets, claves, gong, marimba, bells and chimes.

group.title: Instruments of the band and orchestra

results.title: Introduction
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Instruments of the band and orchestra”.
Shows the three sections of a band - the brasses, the woodwinds, and the percussions - and explains the function of each group of instruments. Explains that the addition of a string section distinguishes an orchestra from a band. Includes demonstrations of well-known instruments from each section. Features the Jollet Township High School Band and the Youth Orchestra of Greater Chicago.

results.title: Early handling of spinal injuries
results.creator_credit: Walford Hewitson and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description:
Episode number 7 of Series “Accidents don’t happen”.
Shows what to do and what not to do in the way of on-the-spot first aid when industrial back injuries occur. Animation illustrates the structure of the spinal column and how paralysis may result from injury to the inner spinal cord. Detailed demonstrations, both in a factory and in a logging camp, show how an accident victim must be handled in order to avoid more serious complications.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Walford Hewitson and National Film Board of Canada

results.title: Handling
results.creator_credit: Donald Mulholland, Fred Lasse and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description:
Episode number 3 of Series “Accidents don’t happen”.
A brief quiz on how, or how not, to handle heavy loads. In a two-minute test the audience is invited to spot nine mistakes in handling methods. Men are seen working with packing cases, cement sacks, oil drums and metal rods - and having trouble. After the test, the same workers are shown doing their jobs the right way.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Donald Mulholland, Fred Lasse and National Film Board of Canada

results.title: Machines
results.creator_credit: Donald Mulholland, Fred Lasse and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description:
Episode number 2 of Series “Accidents don’t happen”.
Minor carelessness in handling machinery may lead to a major accident. Investigating a fatal accident involving a power saw, the film reconstructs the steps leading up to the fatality and shows the worker’s two key mistakes in handling the saw.
